action: take only the red ball from a table while leaving the blue ball untouched

subject.description: one person standing or sitting at a table

scene.description: simple tabletop scene with one red ball and one blue ball

initial_state: One red ball and one blue ball sit separately on the table. The person has not touched either ball yet.

stages.description: The person is near the table. The red ball and blue ball both remain on the table and are untouched.
The person reaches specifically toward the red ball while the blue ball remains untouched on the table.
The person grasps or lifts the red ball. The blue ball remains in its original position on the table.
The person holds only the red ball away from the table. The blue ball is still on the table and has not moved.

temporal_constraints: Both balls must start on the table.
The person must reach toward the red ball before lifting it.
The blue ball must remain on the table through all four stages.
Only the red ball may change location.
causal_constraints: The red ball moves because the person's hand contacts and lifts it.
The blue ball has no causal trigger and therefore must remain unchanged.
forbidden_violations: The blue ball is picked up, moved, removed, or changed color.
The red and blue balls swap identities.
The red ball appears in the hand before contact.
Either ball is duplicated or disappears without cause.

reference_image_requirements.style: realistic photo, no text, no labels

notes: This case tests selective manipulation, quantity preservation, object identity, and counterfactual constraint sensitivity.
